speech. Is it right that a Judge
shall take away a mans right of
speech? If be speaks the truth
he is going to hurt the business.


"The court of appeals disagreed
then the United States court de-
cided labor unions are a trust. A
trust is a monopolization of the
products of labor. Unions are COM
binations of laborers. The courts
decided that business is entitled
to damages and if the organiza-
ton has not enough to pay them,
then they can levy against the in
dividuals. We are going back to
the times of Henry the XVIII.


Sunny jim Sherman said be-
fore the close of congress, We are
not only responsible for what we
do. but what we don't do


1--WE went to Chicago and there
they gave us the whip and sent us
to Denver. There we presented
the same propositions and they
were adopted by the democrats.
And that is why organized labor
will support the democrats.


Toward the close of the address
Thomas Cavanaugh, former city
inspector and now holding a job
as bailiff in the federal court. in
terrupted and took issue with the
speaker, contending that injunc-
lions were only against picketing
and that the speakers ideas were
wrong.


Mr.. Furuseth's reply was brief
and hot and Cavanaugh left the
hall Immediately afterward, follow
ed by derisive shouts. Furuseth
said to Cavanaugh: 1--WE only de-
mand the same right that would
be accorded man who would muir
der your wife or children"
